meaning: adj.未订正的,未修正的,未加管教和,未调整的
examples: Prices — uncorrected for inflation — are now at record highs.
Uncorrected refractive errors (myopia, hyperopia or astigmatism), 43 %.
She pushed Ted to correct the mistakes several times, but there were always some uncorrected errors left.
Medical journals frequently fail to perform basic checks on the papers they print, so all sorts of sharp practice goes uncorrected.
How little respect does the New York Post have for its readers that it allows such an obvious and glaring inaccuracy to go uncorrected?
